author: barnabasbusa
content: <@586161934425128960> are you sure that we can't just use a static flag on the EL similar to how it is done right now ?

author: nflaig
content:
so right now builders need to adhere to the gas limit the proposer wants, eg. in lodestar we reject a header if it does not follow that. In gloas, it will still be the same, the proposer signals what gas limit they want via proposer preferences and builder should adhere to that

the only relevant cause is if you want to use a vanilla CL and EL and act as a builder which is pretty niche

aside that though, having the gas limit only configured on the CL is nice in general, current UX is pretty horrible and confusing

author: nflaig
content: maybe <@755590043632140352> can confirm, but I am pretty confident we would need the CL to signal the gas limit per block to the EL

author: potuz
content: yes, we've been discussing this forever, and there are two currents here, some EL devs (specially in Nethermind) want to make this client decided and not validator decided and just hardcode this in EL clients, this way core devs can decide what the gas limit is. Others want validators to decide this and we keep complicating paths by allowing the EL to control this. Everything points to the CL having to pass this to the EL in a per-block status even to have good circuit breakers. cc <@592004585506340885> that has been designing reasonable circuit breakers to trigger automatically. In a world in which the external builders fail and we can't really build locally at the current gas limit, we need the CL to start signaling lower gas limits on each block and start producing smaller blocks. The only reason we didn not include this in the first EIP design is that I wanted to have the selling point of "this does not require **any** changes on the engine API nor the EL"
